overview: The deck moves from the failure mode of naive long-form generation to a structured, multi-stage agent design for screenplays, closing with how that pipeline generalizes.

acts.name: Problem: long-form generation limits
acts.function: Frame and develop ideas in this section of the deck.
acts.summary: Why one-shot writing drifts and loses coherence.
acts.why_this_section_exists: Students need a crisp failure mode before any architectural fix.

acts.name: Design: hierarchical screenplay / agent pipeline
acts.function: Frame and develop ideas in this section of the deck.
acts.summary: Premise, arc, and scene-level agents for structured output.
acts.why_this_section_exists: Once the problem is credible, the lecture introduces the decomposition that addresses it.

acts.name: Synthesis: end-to-end flow and takeaways
acts.function: Frame and develop ideas in this section of the deck.
acts.summary: How the pieces connect and generalize beyond screenplays.
acts.why_this_section_exists: The closing ties mechanism back to transferable document design.

transitions.transition_reason: The deck shifts from Problem: long-form generation limits to Design: hierarchical screenplay / agent pipeline so students do not accept tooling before they accept the coherence problem it solves.
transitions.pedagogical_logic: Problem motivation must precede architectural prescription.

transitions.transition_reason: The deck shifts from Design: hierarchical screenplay / agent pipeline to Synthesis: end-to-end flow and takeaways so students do not accept tooling before they accept the coherence problem it solves.
transitions.pedagogical_logic: Problem motivation must precede architectural prescription.

ending_function: Students should see long-form generation as a planning problem solvable with staged agents.
